title: Lipid follow-up
population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
People receiving laboratory monitoring for listed chronic conditions, including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hyperlipidaemia.
settings: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: INTRODUCTION Health Regulation Sector (HRS) forms an integral part of Dubai Health Authority (DHA) and is mandated by DHA Law No. (14) of the year (2021) amending some clauses of law No. (6) of 2018 pertaining to the Dubai Health Authority (DHA), to undertake several functions including but not limited to: • Developing regulation, policy, standards, guidelines to improve quality and patient safety
heading_path: Page 2

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: • Pioneering prevention efforts against non-communicable diseases. • Become a global digital health hub. • Foster healthcare education, research and innovation. ACKNOWLEDGMENT The Health Policy and Standards Department (HPSD) developed this Guideline in collaboration with Subject Matter Experts and would like to acknowledge and thank these health professionals for their dedication toward improving
heading_path: Page 3

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY Telehealth is based on Evidence Based Practice (EBP) which is the conscientious, explicit and judicious use of current best evidence in making decisions about the care of the individual patient. It means integrating individual clinical expertise with the best available external clinical evidence and guidelines from systematic research. EBP is important because it aims to provide
heading_path: Page 5

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: DEFINITIONS/ABBREVIATIONS Virtual Clinical Assessment: Is the evaluation of the patient's medical condition virtually via telephone or video call consultations, which may include one or more of the following: patient medical history, physical examination and diagnostic investigations. Patient: The person who receives the healthcare services or the medical investigation or treatment provided by a D

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 1. BACKGROUND 1.1. This guideline outlines the type of laboratory tests that need to be done for some chronic conditions before doctors prescribe and/or adjust the dosages of patients’ chronic medications. 1.2. It should be noted that Chronic/Repeat Medications only to be prescribed after a video-consultation with a patient. Doctors must ascertain that the precise list of patients’ medications, in
heading_path: Page 8

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 4.2. Exclusion for Telehealth services are as follows 4.2.1. Emergency cases where immediate intervention or referral is required. 4.2.2. Prescribe Narcotics, Controlled or Semi-Controlled medications. 5. RECOMMENDATION 5.1. Chronic conditions that could be managed virtually Some of the chronic diseases/ conditions that could be managed virtually include (but are not limited to) the following: 5.1
heading_path: Page 9

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 5.1.14. Dyspepsia (Gastritis/duodenitis)/GORD 5.1.15. IBD (Ulcerative Colitis or Chon’s Disease) 5.1.16. Chronic back pain 5.1.17. Vitamin D deficiency 5.1.18. Osteoporosis 5.1.19. Peripheral Vascular Disease (PVD) 5.1.20. Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) 5.1.21. Old Stroke 5.1.22. Other conditions to be managed and repeat medications to be prescribed on doctor’s discretion on case-by-case (note: doct
heading_path: Page 10

locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 5.2.5. To determine if it is possible to stop treatment 5.2.6. Based on doctor’s clinical judgment 5.3. Refer to APPENDIX 1 for Required Laboratory Tests. 5.4. Refer to APPENDIX 2 for Drugs Monitoring and Tests to be done before issuing Repeat Prescriptions. Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases Code: DHA/HRS/HPSD/CG-32 Issue Nu: 2 Issue Date: 21/02/2

Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: NICE guideline Reference number: NG238 Published: 14 December 2023 Download guidance (PDF)

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
On this page
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: 1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease 1.2 Aspirin for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease 1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease 1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review 1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins 1.6 Lipid-lowering

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For the primary prevention of cardiovascular disease (CVD) in primary care, use a systematic strategy to identify people who are likely to be at high risk of CVD. [2008, amended 2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Prioritise people based on an estimate of their CVD risk before doing a full formal risk assessment. Estimate their CVD risk using CVD risk factors already recorded in primary care electronic medical records. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Review estimates of CVD risk on an ongoing basis for people over 40. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Prioritise people for a full formal risk assessment if their estimated 10‑year risk of CVD is 10% or more. [2008, amended 2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Discuss the process of risk assessment with the person identified as being at risk, including the option of declining any formal risk assessment. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Identifying people for full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use opportunistic assessment as the main strategy in primary care to identify CVD risk in unselected people. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use the QRISK3 tool to calculate the estimated CVD risk within the next 10 years for people aged between 25 and 84 without CVD. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use the QRISK3 tool for people with type 2 diabetes aged between 25 and 84. [May 2023] Practical information about using the QRISK3 tool Until electronic clinical systems in which QRISK2 is embedded are updated with QRISK3, it may be necessary to use QRISK2. When assessing risk for people taking corticosteroids or atypical antipsychotics or people with systemic lupus erythematosus, migraine, sever

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use a risk assessment tool for people who are at high risk of CVD, including people with: type 1 diabetes (see the section on primary prevention of CVD for people with type 1 diabetes ) an estimated glomerular filtration rate less than 60 ml per minute per 1.73 m 2 and/or albuminuria (see the section on primary and secondary prevention of CVD for people with chronic kidney disease [CKD] ) f

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Recognise that CVD risk tools may underestimate risk in certain groups of people, including but not limited to: people treated for HIV people already taking medicines to treat CVD risk factors people who have recently stopped smoking people taking medicines that can cause dyslipidaemia, such as immunosuppressant drugs people with severe mental illness people with autoimmune disorders, and other sy

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Full formal risk assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider people aged 85 or older to be at increased risk of CVD because of age alone, particularly people who smoke or have raised blood pressure. [May 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations The committee agreed that the evidence suggested QRISK3 performed best among tools evaluated in a UK population to assess the risk of a person without established CVD having a CVD event within the

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Follow the recommendations on communication in NICE's guidelines on patient experience in adult NHS services and shared decision making . [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Set aside adequate time during the consultation to provide information on risk assessment and to answer any questions. Arrange for further consultation if needed. [2008, amended May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Document the discussion relating to the consultation on risk assessment and the person's decision. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer people information about their absolute risk of CVD and the absolute benefits and harms of any intervention over a 10‑year period. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider using a lifetime risk tool such as QRISK3-lifetime to inform discussions on CVD risk and to motivate lifestyle changes, particularly for people with a 10‑year QRISK3 score less than 10%, and people under 40 who have CVD risk factors. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: To encourage the person to participate in reducing their CVD risk: find out what, if anything, the person has already been told about their CVD risk and how they feel about it explore the person's beliefs about what determines future health (this may affect their attitude to changing risk) assess their readiness to make changes to their lifestyle (diet, physical activity, smoking and alcohol consu

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.1 Identifying and assessing cardiovascular disease risk for people without established cardiovascular disease
Communication about risk assessment, lifestyle changes and treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the person's CVD risk is at a level where treatment is recommended but they decline the offer of treatment, advise them that their CVD risk should be reassessed in the future. Record their choice in their medical records. [2008, amended 2014] Why the committee made these recommendations The committee agreed that the evidence did not support using lifetime CVD risk assessment tools to guide deci

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.2 Aspirin for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not routinely offer aspirin for primary prevention of CVD. [January 2023] For guidance on using aspirin to prevent venous thromboembolism in over 16s in hospital, see NICE's guideline on venous thromboembolism in over 16s: reducing the risk of hospital-acquired deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism . Why the committee made this recommendation NICE's surveillance team reviewed the evidence

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Behaviour change
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise and support people at high risk of or with CVD to achieve a healthy lifestyle in line with NICE's guideline on behaviour change: general approaches . [2014, amended May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Healthy eating
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For advice on healthy eating, see the NHS eat well guide .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Cardioprotective diet
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people at high risk of or with CVD to eat a diet in which total fat intake is 30% or less of total energy intake, saturated fats are 7% or less of total energy intake, and where possible saturated fats are replaced by mono‑unsaturated and polyunsaturated fats. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Cardioprotective diet
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people at high risk of or with CVD to: reduce their saturated fat intake increase their mono-unsaturated fat intake with olive oil, rapeseed oil or spreads based on these oils and to use them in food preparation. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Cardioprotective diet
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Take account of a person's individual circumstances, for example, drug treatment, comorbidities and other lifestyle changes, when giving dietary advice. [2014] Why the committee made these recommendations There was no available evidence comparing the effectiveness of dietary cholesterol strategies with normal diets for adults with and without CVD, so the committee updated the 2014 recommendation b

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Physical activity
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people at high risk of or with CVD to do aerobic and muscle-strengthening activities in line with the UK Chief Medical Officers' physical activity guidelines . [2008, amended 2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Physical activity
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Encourage people who are unable to perform moderate intensity physical activity because of comorbidity, medical conditions or personal circumstances to exercise at their maximum safe capacity. [2008, amended 2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Physical activity
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advice about physical activity should take into account the person's needs, preferences and circumstances. Agree goals and provide the person with written information about the benefits of activity and local opportunities to be active, in line with recommendation 2 of NICE's guideline on physical activity: brief advice for adults . [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Physical activity
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Follow recommendation 8 of NICE's guideline on walking and cycling , and recommendation 2 of NICE's guideline on exercise referral schemes . [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Weight management
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer people at high risk of or with CVD who are overweight or obese appropriate interventions in line with NICE's guideline on overweight and obesity management . [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Alcohol consumption
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For advice on how to keep the health risks from drinking alcohol to a low level, see the UK Chief Medical Officer's alcohol consumption guidelines . [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Smoking cessation
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise and support all people who smoke to stop, in line with the recommendations on treating tobacco dependence in NICE's guideline on tobacco . [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.3 Lifestyle changes for the primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Plant stanols and sterols
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not advise any of the following to take plant stanols or sterols to prevent CVD: people being treated for primary prevention people being treated for secondary prevention people with CKD people with type 1 diabetes people with type 2 diabetes. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure both total blood cholesterol and high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol to achieve the best estimate of CVD risk. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use clinical findings, a full lipid profile and family history to judge the likelihood of a familial lipid disorder, rather than using strict lipid cut-off values alone. [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Exclude possible common secondary causes of dyslipidaemia (such as excess alcohol intake, uncontrolled diabetes, hypothyroidism, liver disease and nephrotic syndrome) before referring for specialist review. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use the recommendations in NICE's guideline on familial hypercholesterolaemia to determine whether to suspect, and how to treat, familial hypercholesterolaemia. [2014, amended May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Arrange for specialist assessment of people with a total blood cholesterol level of more than 9.0 mmol per litre or a non-HDL cholesterol level of more than 7.5 mmol per litre even in the absence of a first-degree family history of premature coronary heart disease. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Refer for urgent specialist review if a person has a triglyceride level of more than 20 mmol per litre that is not a result of excess alcohol intake or poor glycaemic control. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In people with a triglyceride level between 10 mmol and 20 mmol per litre: repeat the triglyceride measurement with a fasting test (after an interval of 5 days, but within 2 weeks) and review for potential secondary causes of hyperlipidaemia and seek specialist advice if the triglyceride level remains at more than 10 mmol per litre. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.4 Initial lipid measurement and referral for specialist review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In people with a triglyceride level between 4.5 mmol and 9.9 mmol per litre: be aware that the CVD risk may be underestimated by risk assessment tools and optimise the management of other CVD risk factors present and seek specialist advice if non-HDL cholesterol level is more than 7.5 mmol per litre. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Discuss risks and benefits of statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Make decisions about starting statin treatment after an informed discussion between the clinician and the person about the risks and benefits of statins. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Discuss risks and benefits of statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Take into account potential benefits from lifestyle changes, the person's preferences, the presence of any comorbidities, whether they are on multiple medications, whether they are frail and their life expectancy. (See also NICE's guideline on multimorbidity .) [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Discuss risks and benefits of statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people who are being offered a statin that the risk of muscle pain, tenderness or weakness associated with statin use is small and the rate of severe muscle adverse effects (rhabdomyolysis) because of statins is extremely low. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Discuss possible interactions between statins and other substances
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people who are being treated with a statin: that other drugs, some foods (for example, grapefruit juice) and some supplements may interfere with statins and to always consult the patient information leaflet, a pharmacist or prescriber for advice when starting other drugs or thinking about taking supplements. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Perform baseline blood tests and clinical assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before starting statins perform baseline blood tests and clinical assessment. Include all of the following in the assessment: smoking status alcohol consumption blood pressure (see NICE's guideline on hypertension in adults ) BMI or other measure of obesity (see NICE's guideline on overweight and obesity management ) full lipid profile diabetes status renal function transaminase level (alanine ami

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Perform baseline blood tests and clinical assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not routinely exclude from statin treatment people who have liver transaminase levels that are raised but are less than 3 times the upper limit of normal. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Perform baseline blood tests and clinical assessment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before offering a statin, ask the person if they have had persistent generalised unexplained muscle symptoms (pain, tenderness or weakness), whether associated or not with previous lipid-lowering treatment. If they have, measure creatine kinase levels. If creatine kinase levels are: more than 5 times the upper limit of normal, re-measure creatine kinase after 7 days; if creatine kinase levels are

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Choice of drug based on clinical trials
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware that when deciding on lipid-lowering treatment to prevent CVD, drugs are preferred for which there is evidence in clinical trials of a beneficial effect on CVD morbidity and mortality. [2008]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Statins and pregnancy
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware that statins are contraindicated in pregnancy because of the risk to the unborn child of exposure to statins. [2014, amended May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.5 Discussions and assessment before starting statins
Statins and pregnancy
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Explain that: statins should be stopped if pregnancy is a possibility statins should be stopped 3 months before attempting to conceive statins should not be restarted until breastfeeding is finished. [2014, amended May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: There is a NICE patient decision aid to support discussions about statin treatment to reduce the risk of heart disease and stroke for people without CVD . Also see the sections on optimising statin treatment and assessing response to treatment .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Lipid target for people taking statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For primary prevention of CVD aim for a greater than 40% reduction in non-HDL cholesterol. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Optimising lifestyle changes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before offering statin treatment for primary prevention, discuss the benefits of lifestyle changes and optimise the management of all other modifiable CVD risk factors if possible. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Optimising lifestyle changes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Recognise that people may need support to change their lifestyle. To help them do this, refer them to programmes such as exercise referral schemes or weight management services. (See NICE's guidelines on behaviour change: individual approaches , physical activity: exercise referral schemes and overweight and obesity management .) [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Optimising lifestyle changes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer people the opportunity to have their risk of CVD assessed again after they have tried to change their lifestyle. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Optimising lifestyle changes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If lifestyle change is ineffective or inappropriate offer statin treatment. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Treating comorbidities and secondary causes of dyslipidaemia
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before starting statins, treat comorbidities and secondary causes of dyslipidaemia. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with and without type 2 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer atorvastatin 20 mg for the primary prevention of CVD to people who have a 10‑year QRISK3 score of 10% or more. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with and without type 2 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not rule out treatment with atorvastatin 20 mg for the primary prevention of CVD just because the person's 10‑year QRISK3 score is less than 10% if they have an informed preference for taking a statin or there is concern that risk may be underestimated. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with and without type 2 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For people aged 85 and older consider treatment with atorvastatin 20 mg. Be aware of factors that may make treatment inappropriate (see recommendations 1.5.1 and 1.5.2 ). [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with type 1 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer statin treatment for the primary prevention of CVD to adults with type 1 diabetes who: are older than 40 years or have had diabetes for more than 10 years or have established nephropathy or have other CVD risk factors. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with type 1 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider statin treatment for the primary prevention of CVD for people aged 18 to 40 with type 1 diabetes, including those who have had diabetes for 10 years or less. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with type 1 diabetes
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When starting treatment with a statin for adults with type 1 diabetes, use atorvastatin 20 mg. [May 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations Evidence on both the effectiveness and adverse effects of statins showed high-intensity statins are clinically effective and cost effective compared to no statins, low-intensity statins , or medium-intensity statins for preventing CVD in people with

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Statin treatment for people with type 1 diabetes
Statins and QRISK score
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Evidence showed that statins are cost effective for people with 10‑year CVD risk scores less than 10%. The committee agreed that if more people took statins there would be a greater reduction in CVD events. However, they also recognised that practical considerations needed to be taken into account. They agreed that risk scores are an important aid to shared decision making on statins. National aud

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.8 Statins for primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease in people with chronic kidney disease
Optimising statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See the section on optimising statin treatment .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
Assessing response to treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See the section on assessing response to treatment .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.6 Lipid-lowering treatment for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Ezetimibe, co-administered with a statin, is recommended as an option in NICE technology appraisal guidance for treating primary hypercholesterolaemia in some adults who have started statin therapy when serum total or low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol concentration is not appropriately controlled. For full details, see the guidance on ezetimibe (TA385, 2016) .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: These recommendations apply to people with and without type 1 and 2 diabetes. Also see the sections on optimising statin treatment and assessing response to treatment .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Lipid target for people taking lipid-lowering treatments
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: For secondary prevention of CVD, aim for LDL cholesterol levels of 2.0 mmol per litre or less, or non-HDL cholesterol levels of 2.6 mmol per litre or less. [December 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
Lipid target
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The committee agreed LDL cholesterol and non-HDL cholesterol levels should be reduced as much as possible in people with CVD. However, people respond differently to statins and other lipid-lowering treatments, and it is not cost effective to offer the full range of treatments to everyone with CVD. The clinical evidence consisted of 34 randomised control trials (RCTs). Clinically significant reduct

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
Statin plus ezetimibe
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: In a separate analysis, escalation with statin plus ezetimibe (but no injectable treatment) was evaluated at different LDL cholesterol levels. Ezetimibe was cost effective regardless of LDL cholesterol, so the committee agreed that it could be considered for people with lipid levels below the agreed target. They noted that the trade-off between further reducing risk and increasing medication shoul

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Initial treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer atorvastatin 80 mg to people with CVD, whatever their cholesterol level, unless the person meets the criteria in recommendation 1.7.3. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Initial treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer a lower dose of atorvastatin if any of the following apply: it could react with other drugs there is a high risk of adverse effects the person would prefer to take a lower dose. [May 2023, amended December 2023] In December 2023, this was an off-label use of atorvastatin. See NICE's information on prescribing medicines .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Initial treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not delay statin treatment for secondary prevention of CVD but discuss lifestyle changes at the same time if appropriate. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Initial treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If a person has acute coronary syndrome, do not delay statin treatment. Measure full lipid profile on admission and at 2 to 3 months after starting treatment. [May 2023, amended December 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations Evidence on both the effectiveness and adverse effects of statins showed high-intensity statins are clinically effective and cost effective compared to no statins

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Treating comorbidities and secondary causes of dyslipidaemia
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Treat comorbidities and secondary causes of dyslipidaemia at the same time as starting statin treatment. [December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Make decisions about escalating lipid-lowering treatment after an informed discussion between the clinician and the person about the risks and benefits of additional lipid-lowering treatments. [December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Take into account the person's preferences, the presence of any comorbidities, whether they are on multiple medications, whether they are frail and their life expectancy. (See also NICE's guideline on multimorbidity .) [December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the person is taking the maximum tolerated dose and intensity of statin but the lipid target for secondary prevention of CVD is not met (see recommendation 1.7.1 ), consider additional lipid-lowering treatments recommended as options in NICE's technology appraisal guidance on: alirocumab (TA393, June 2016) evolocumab (TA394, June 2016) ezetimibe (TA385, February 2016) inclisiran (TA733, October

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.7 Lipid-lowering treatment for secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
Escalating treatment for people on statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider ezetimibe in addition to the maximum tolerated intensity and dose of statin to reduce CVD risk further, even if the lipid target for secondary prevention of CVD is met (see recommendation 1.7.1). [December 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.8 Statins for primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease in people with chronic kidney disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: See NICE's guideline on chronic kidney disease for CKD classification. People on renal replacement therapy are outside the scope of this guideline.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.8 Statins for primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease in people with chronic kidney disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer atorvastatin 20 mg for the primary or secondary prevention of CVD to people with CKD. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.8 Statins for primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease in people with chronic kidney disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the lipid target for primary or secondary prevention of CVD (see recommendation 1.6.1 and recommendation 1.7.1 ) is not met and eGFR is 30 ml per minute per 1.73 m 2 or more, increase the dose of atorvastatin. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.8 Statins for primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease in people with chronic kidney disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Agree the use of higher doses with a renal specialist if eGFR is less than 30 ml per minute per 1.73 m 2 . [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.9 Optimising statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the lipid target for primary or secondary prevention of CVD (see recommendation 1.6.1 and recommendation 1.7.1 ) is not met: discuss adherence and timing of statin dose with the person encourage them to continue improvements to their diet and lifestyle, and to make further changes if appropriate consider increasing the statin intensity/dose if the person is not currently taking a high-intensity

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.9 Optimising statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the person reports adverse effects when taking a high-intensity statin, discuss the following strategies with them: stopping the statin and trying again when the symptoms have resolved to check if the symptoms are related to the statin changing to a different statin in the same intensity group (rosuvastatin if already receiving atorvastatin) reducing the dose changing to a lower-intensity stati

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.9 Optimising statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If a person is not able to tolerate a high-intensity statin, aim to treat with the maximum tolerated intensity and dose of statin. [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.9 Optimising statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise the person that any statin at any dose reduces CVD risk. [2014, amended May 2023 and December 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations The committee amended the 2014 consensus recommendation on what to do if someone taking statins does not reach their lipid target to apply to both the existing primary prevention target and the new secondary prevention target. The recommended actio

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Also see the section on assessing response to treatment .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Ezetimibe is recommended as an option in NICE technology appraisal guidance for treating primary hypercholesterolaemia in adults if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated. For full details, see the guidance on ezetimibe (TA385, 2016) .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Bempedoic acid with ezetimibe is recommended as an option in NICE technology appraisal guidance for treating primary hypercholesterolaemia or mixed dyslipidaemia if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated, and ezetimibe alone does not control LDL cholesterol well enough. For full details, see the guidance on bempedoic acid with ezetimibe (TA694, 2021) .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
Secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer ezetimibe instead of a statin to people for whom statins are contraindicated or, if after documented discussion of the strategies outlined in recommendations 1.9.2 and 1.9.3 , it is recognised the person cannot tolerate statins of any intensity or dose. This applies whatever the person's cholesterol level. Ezetimibe is recommended as an option in NICE technology appraisal guidance for treati

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.10 Treatment if statins are contraindicated or not tolerated
Secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If the person is taking ezetimibe but the lipid target for secondary prevention is not met (see recommendation 1.7.1 ), consider alternative or additional lipid-lowering treatments recommended as options in NICE's technology appraisal guidance on: alirocumab (TA393, June 2016) bempedoic acid with ezetimibe (TA694, April 2021) evolocumab (TA394, June 2016) inclisiran (TA733, October 2021) . See the

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
When to repeat blood tests
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure liver transaminase and full lipid profile at 2 to 3 months after starting or changing lipid-lowering treatment. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
When to repeat blood tests
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Measure liver transaminase at 12 months, but not again unless clinically indicated. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
When to measure creatine kinase
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise people who are being treated with a statin to seek medical advice if they develop unexplained muscle symptoms (pain, tenderness or weakness). If this occurs, measure creatine kinase. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
When to measure creatine kinase
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If people report muscle pain, tenderness or weakness while taking a statin and have a creatine kinase level less than 5 times the upper limit of normal, reassure them that their symptoms are unlikely to be due to the statin and explore other possible causes. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
When to measure creatine kinase
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not measure creatine kinase levels in asymptomatic people who are being treated with a statin. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Increase in blood glucose or HbA1c
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not stop statins because of an increase in blood glucose level or HbA1c. (See the recommendations on assessing for risk of diabetes mellitus in NICE's guideline on preventing type 2 diabetes .) [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Restarting statins
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Remind the person to restart the statin if they stopped taking it because of drug interactions or to treat intercurrent illnesses. [May 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Annual medication review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Provide annual medication reviews for people on lipid-lowering treatment. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Annual medication review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer an annual full lipid profile to inform discussions about secondary prevention of CVD. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Annual medication review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider an annual full lipid profile to inform discussions about primary prevention of CVD. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Annual medication review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: During the annual medication review: discuss and encourage medicines adherence, if the shared decision is to continue with lipid-lowering treatment discuss and encourage dietary and lifestyle changes if appropriate address CVD risk factors. [May 2023, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.11 Assessing response to treatment
Annual medication review
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Discuss with people who are stable on a low-intensity statin or medium‑intensity statin the likely benefits and potential risks of changing to a high-intensity statin when they have a medication review and agree with the person whether a change is needed. [May 2023] Why the committee made these recommendations The committee agreed that more flexibility in the timing of blood tests to measure lipid

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: These recommendations apply to primary and secondary prevention of CVD, including people with diabetes and CKD. They do not apply to people with familial hypercholesterolaemia. For guidance on using fibrates, bile acid sequestrants and combination treatment for this population group, follow the recommendations on drug treatment in NICE's guideline on familial hypercholesterolaemia .

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Adherence to statin treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer coenzyme Q10 or vitamin D to increase adherence to statin treatment. [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Fibrates
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not routinely offer fibrates to prevent CVD. [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Nicotinic acid
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer nicotinic acid (niacin) to prevent CVD. [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Bile acid sequestrants (anion exchange resins)
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer a bile acid sequestrant (anion exchange resin) to prevent CVD. [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Omega 3 fatty acid compounds
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not offer omega 3 fatty acid compounds to prevent CVD. Icosapent ethyl is an exception to this if used as described in NICE's technology appraisal guidance on icosapent ethyl with statin therapy for reducing the risk of cardiovascular events in people with raised triglycerides (TA805, 2022) . [2014, amended December 2023]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Omega 3 fatty acid compounds
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Tell people that there is no evidence that omega 3 fatty acid compounds help to prevent CVD, except use of icosapent ethyl as described in NICE's technology appraisal guidance on icosapent ethyl with statin therapy for reducing the risk of cardiovascular events in people with raised triglycerides (TA805, 2022) . [2014]

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
1.12 Lipid-lowering treatments that should not be used or not used routinely
Combination treatment
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: To prevent CVD, do not offer the combination of a statin with: a bile acid sequestrant (anion exchange resin), a fibrate or nicotinic acid or an omega 3 fatty acid compound, except icosapent ethyl as described in NICE's technology appraisal guidance on icosapent ethyl with statin therapy for reducing the risk of cardiovascular events in people with raised triglycerides (TA805, 2022) . [2014, amend

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: This section defines terms that have been used in a particular way for this guideline.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
Full lipid profile
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: This involves taking a blood sample to measure total cholesterol, HDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels and then calculating non-HDL cholesterol and LDL cholesterol (a fasting sample is not mandated). LDL cholesterol results may not be reported in participants with triglyceride levels more than 4.5 mmol per litre or 9 mmol per litre depending on the formula used by local laboratories.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
High-intensity statin
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The following doses for statins are high intensity, based on the percentage reduction in LDL cholesterol they can produce: atorvastatin: 20 mg to 80 mg rosuvastatin: 10 mg to 40 mg.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
Medium-intensity statin
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The following doses for statins are medium intensity, based on the percentage reduction in LDL cholesterol they can produce: atorvastatin: 10 mg fluvastatin: 80 mg rosuvastatin: 5 mg simvastatin: 20 mg to 40 mg.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
Low-intensity statin
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The following doses for statins are low intensity, based on the percentage reduction in LDL cholesterol they can produce: fluvastatin: 20 mg to 40 mg pravastatin: 5 mg to 40 mg simvastatin: 10 mg.

heading_path: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
Recommendations
Terms used in this guideline
Severe mental illness
locator.paragraph_or_text_span: A diagnosis of schizophrenia, bipolar disorder or other psychoses. (In line with the criteria for severe mental health conditions used in the NHS annual health check for people with severe mental health conditions .)
